overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong and highly engaged academic presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, with a collective following of 3,580. His content successfully bridges the gap between complex computational social science research and professional academic opportunities, yielding high interaction rates relative to follower counts across all platforms.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of research papers and software updates, such as CooRnet v2.0, focusing on detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Job openings and research opportunities, including postdoctoral hiring and PhD student recruitment in computational social science.
Sharing research grant achievements and upcoming presentations at specialized events like DisinfoConf2025.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on LinkedIn to capitalize on its high absolute engagement and large professional follower base.
Embed direct calls-to-action (such as application links or paper PDFs) within academic recruitment and publication posts to convert high engagement into direct results.
Cross-promote highly engaging Bluesky threads on LinkedIn and Mastodon to unify the academic audience across decentralized platforms.
Leverage visual media, such as charts or tool interface screenshots, to boost the visibility of research software releases like CooRnet.

platformInsights.bluesky: Yields the highest relative engagement rate (6.14% average), demonstrating a highly active and receptive audience particularly eager for publication news and academic opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Hosts a niche, highly technical community on the aoir.social instance, showing strong interest in open-source tool updates and research methodologies.
platformInsights.linkedin: Represents the largest audience share (1,850 followers) and drives the highest absolute volume of engagements, performing exceptionally well on milestone announcements such as research grants and job openings.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
